_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot, Adam-supervised)
_meta.note: LOCAL refinement batch — Chesapeake VA: 4 City Council members (Bunn, Jefferies, Smith, King) + Commonwealth's Attorney (Hamel). Roster verified June 2026. All TRUE/FALSE cite a source URL; null where no evidence. Prior speculative party-default cells reset to null.

profile.religion: Member, Raleigh Heights Baptist Church (South Norfolk, SBC)
profile.education: Great Bridge High School; Tidewater Community College; TMP (Tourism Meeting Professional), Southeast Tourism Marketing College
profile.background: Native Chesapeake; 37-year retired Chesapeake city employee (Parks/Recreation/Tourism and Economic Development); Realtor with Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices / RW Towne Realty. Prior office: Chesapeake School Board 2006–2020 (appointed 2006; elected 2008, 2012, 2016; served as Vice Chair and Chair). Elected At-Large to Chesapeake City Council Nov 8, 2022 with backing of Republican Party of Chesapeake; sworn in Jan 1, 2023; term ends Jan 1, 2027; up for reelection November 2026. Civic: Chesapeake Rotary; South Norfolk Civic League; Great Bridge / Chesapeake Jaycees; Governor's School for the Arts Regional Board (Vice Chair).

jeff-bunn.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Chesapeake council member, sworn in 1/1/2023, term ends 1/1/2027, up for reelection 11/2026. Republican-Party-of-Chesapeake-endorsed in 2022 (slate with Newins, Whitaker, Ennis, Knowles, Vitale). HARD evidence: (1) personally delivered the council invocation at the Feb 14, 2023 Work Session (his first month in office) per official minutes; member of Raleigh Heights Baptist Church (SBC) — christian_liberty q3 TRUE; (2) voted YES on unanimous 9-0 FY24 budget that CUT real-estate tax rate 4 cents ($1.05 → $1.01); voted YES on FY26 $1.7B budget; sponsored 6-1-passed FY26 supplemental allocation to the sheriff's victim/witness program — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE; (3) explicit campaign pledge: 'supporting public safety and giving them the tools to be successful is critical'; sponsored victim/witness-program funding amendment — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative LE-advocacy bar met with concrete sponsored amendment); (4) sponsoring the sheriff's victim/witness program is direct support for victim restitution — public_justice q2 TRUE. DEVIATION FLAG: voted YES on Feb 2026 5-4 toll-rate increase on Chesapeake Expressway / Dominion Boulevard Veterans Bridge (with West, Ritter, Jefferies, Smith; against were Ward, Newins, King, Whitaker — Newins and Whitaker broke from him). 6-3 NO vote on June 2024 ward-system referendum is structural/anti-redistricting and does not map cleanly to election_integrity q0/q1/q4 → left null. NO EVIDENCE on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Mayor West's May 28, 2024 Pride proclamation was mayoral, not a council vote, and Bunn made no public statement), family_child_sovereignty (14-year School Board veteran with only generic 'support students/teachers/staff' rhetoric — too vague), christian_liberty q0/q2/q4, public_justice q1/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (2017 sanctuary-substitute vote predates his council tenure), self_defense (2019 2A Constitutional City declaration predates his council tenure).

3.note: Personally delivered the council invocation at the Feb 14, 2023 Work Session (his first month in office) per official Chesapeake minutes; lifelong member of Raleigh Heights Baptist Church (SBC).

2.note: Voted YES on unanimous 9-0 FY24 budget that CUT real-estate tax rate 4 cents ($1.05 → $1.01); voted YES on FY26 $1.7B budget; sponsored 6-1-passed FY26 amendment funding the sheriff's victim/witness program. Note: voted YES on Feb 2026 5-4 toll-rate increase — fiscal-stewardship deviation flagged in notes.

0.note: Explicit campaign pledge: 'supporting public safety and giving them the tools to be successful is critical'; sponsored FY26 6-1-passed supplemental allocation to the sheriff's victim/witness program — affirmative pro-LE advocacy with a concrete budget commitment.

2.note: Sponsored the FY26 6-1-passed budget amendment funding the sheriff's office victim/witness program — direct, named support for victim restitution and victim-services infrastructure.

profile.background: Ernest Ray 'Jeff' Jefferies Jr.; 23+ year Chesapeake Police Department Captain (Investigations Bureau); co-owner CrossFit Chesapeake; wife is a Chesapeake Public Schools special-education teacher; two daughters CPS graduates. Lost 2024 at-large council race (4th of 9, ~15.07%) on a Republican-Party-of-Chesapeake-aligned platform. Appointed to council Sept 23, 2025 by 7-1 vote (Smith dissenting), nominated by CM Bunn, to fill the seat of Vice Mayor John M. de Triquet (deceased Aug 22, 2025). Term ends Dec 31, 2026; must run November 2026 to retain the seat. No public church/denominational affiliation located.

jeff-jefferies.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: appointed At-Large Chesapeake council member 9/23/2025 (7-1 vote, Smith dissenting), filling late VM de Triquet's seat; term ends 12/31/2026, must run 11/2026 to retain. Career law enforcement — 23-year Chesapeake PD Captain (Investigations). 2024 campaign was Republican-Party-of-Chesapeake-aligned (note: WAVY 'Following the Funds' reported the local GOP was divided over the slate). HARD evidence: (1) explicit 2024 campaign top priority of 'public safety,' grounded in his 23-year Chesapeake PD captain career — public_justice q0 TRUE (career LE officer publicly campaigning on backing public safety meets the affirmative-advocacy bar). Council voting record is thin (~8 months in office, mostly unanimous zoning items). 2024 campaign quote 'Maintaining low taxes, public safety and the best schools in Hampton Roads' is vague low-tax slogan rhetoric, NOT a specific deficit-spending / balanced-budget position → economic_stewardship q2 left null. 2024 campaign opposition to ward-system referendum is structural/citywide-accountability rhetoric and does not map to election_integrity q0/q1/q4 → null. On curbside recycling Jefferies said he was 'open to solutions' / favored 'revisiting the recycling program, citing modern technologies' — open to re-imposing the fee voters rejected — neutral signal, not scored. NO EVIDENCE on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Mayor West's May 28, 2024 first-ever Pride proclamation predates his appointment; no subsequent statement), family_child_sovereignty (wife is CPS teacher; only generic 'best schools' rhetoric), christian_liberty (no invocation delivered in reviewed minutes; no church affiliation publicly stated), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no bail-reform/restitution/death-penalty statement), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (2017 sanctuary-substitute vote and current Sheriff/ICE 287(g) IGSA are not a Jefferies-personal record), self_defense (2019 2A Constitutional City declaration predates his service).

0.note: 23-year Chesapeake Police Department Captain (Investigations Bureau); 2024 council campaign explicit top priority of 'public safety'; Mayor West cited his LE record in nominating him to fill the vacant council seat 9/23/2025 — affirmative-advocacy bar met by career + explicit campaign pledge.

profile.education: B.S. Business Administration, St. Paul's College (1983); M.S. Human Resources Management, University of Richmond
profile.background: Lessie 'Les' Smith Jr.; native Chesapeake; retired Lieutenant, Chesapeake Sheriff's Office (28 years); self-employed in insurance industry; founder of Ground Zero Development Program (nonprofit tutoring young men in Chesapeake). Former Chair, Chesapeake Democratic Committee (currently Chair of its Elections Committee). 4th run for council (lost 2018, 2020, 2022); elected At-Large Nov 5, 2024; took office Jan 1, 2025; term ends Dec 31, 2028. Also serves on the Transportation District Commission of Hampton Roads (2025–). No public church/denominational affiliation located. Disambiguated from the Norfolk WAVY/13NewsNow reporter Les Smith.

les-smith.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Chesapeake council member, sworn in 1/1/2025, term ends 12/31/2028. Party-in-practice openly Democratic (FORMER CHAIR of the Chesapeake Democratic Committee; currently Chair of its Elections Committee; endorsed by Chesapeake Democratic Committee, Sen. Louise Lucas, the Progressive Voters Guide of Virginia, and Gun Sense Voter / Moms Demand Action–Everytown affiliate). HARD evidence: (1) endorsed by GUN SENSE VOTER — Moms Demand Action / Everytown gun-control candidate-endorsement program — direct single-issue evidence of a gun-control posture, opposite of constitutional carry and opposite of opposition to red-flag / mag-limit / AWB / registries → self_defense q0 FALSE and self_defense q1 FALSE; (2) advocated to RESTORE a fee-bearing curbside-recycling program that voters rejected 62-37 in Nov 2024 (endorsed by Chesapeake Recycles for that position); favors increased spending on infrastructure and affordable housing — opposite of opposing deficit/wasteful spending → economic_stewardship q2 FALSE. DEVIATION FLAG: split signal — 28-year Chesapeake Sheriff's Office Lt. retiree (LE biographical), but publicly endorsed by a gun-control PAC and the Progressive Voters Guide. NO EVIDENCE on sanctity_of_life (Progressive Voters Guide listing is general-progressive, not a single-issue abortion endorsement → null), biblical_marriage, family_child_sovereignty, christian_liberty (no invocation, no church publicly identified), election_integrity (ward-system support is structural, does not map to voter-ID/paper-ballot/Zuckerbucks → null), public_justice (no AFFIRMATIVE pro-LE statement located; career LE service is biographical, not advocacy, per the FXBG q0 high-bar rule), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Chesapeake Sheriff's Office IGSAs with ICE are an office-level matter, not Smith's personal stance).

2.note: Campaigned to RESTORE a fee-bearing curbside-recycling program that voters had just rejected 62-37 in the Nov 2024 advisory referendum; endorsed by Chesapeake Recycles for that position; platform favors increased spending on infrastructure and affordable housing — opposite of opposing deficit/wasteful local spending.

0.note: Endorsed by Gun Sense Voter (Moms Demand Action / Everytown gun-control candidate-endorsement program) in the 2024 council race — direct single-issue evidence of a gun-control posture, opposite of constitutional carry.

1.note: Gun Sense Voter (Moms Demand Action / Everytown) endorsement is by definition a gun-control endorsement — opposite of opposing red-flag laws, magazine limits, AWB, and gun registries.

profile.religion: Catholic — parishioner, St. Gregory the Great Catholic Church
profile.education: B.A., College of the Holy Cross; J.D., Rutgers School of Law (admitted VA, NJ, PA)
profile.background: Age 51; career Navy JAG prosecutor and Reserve Commander; Iraq deployment 2007–2008 (Joint Service Commendation Medal); Chesapeake YMCA board; VFW Post 2894 life member. Runs a military-divorce law firm (mildivorce.com). Served on Chesapeake City Council 2018–2021. Originally elected Commonwealth's Attorney as a Republican Nov 2, 2021 (55.4% over Shelly Wood R and Paul Hedges I), took office Jan 1, 2022 — succeeded longtime R CA Nancy G. Parr (16 years, declined to seek re-election). Resigned from Chesapeake GOP Nov 5, 2024 in a published letter to chair Nicholas Proffitt citing party 'deviated from its core principles' and naming former Rep. Randy Forbes / Shirley Forbes. Re-elected as a Democrat Nov 4, 2025, 57%-43% over GOP challenger David Mick. Current term ends Jan 1, 2030; next election Nov 2029.

matthew-hamel.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Chesapeake Commonwealth's Attorney, re-elected 11/4/2025 (57-43 over GOP challenger Mick) as a Democrat after resigning from the Chesapeake GOP 11/5/2024; current term through Jan 1, 2030. HARD evidence: (1) public statement to WAVY/Yahoo (Sept 17, 2025) explicitly framing violent crime in 'public health' terms — 'Breaking the cycle of violent crime requires treating the illness and not just the symptoms' — directly contradicts public_justice q3 → q3 FALSE. Mixed-signal cases that did NOT score: (a) public_justice q0 — created an Elder Abuse Task Force partnering with local + federal LE and explicitly emphasized 'multi-agency task forces,' but no documented FOP / police-union endorsement and a 2025 PAAF (Bill Barr / Tim Griffin's Protecting Americans Action Fund) five-figure ad buy classified him among 'radical progressive prosecutors' for refusing to denounce Jay Jones's 'two bullets to the head' texts about Speaker Gilbert — net null on q0; (b) public_justice q1 — PAAF 'radical progressive prosecutor' classification + GOP challenger Mick's documented allegations (Brady-vacated murder conviction; speedy-trial-dismissed malicious-wounding case; <5% conviction rate on juvenile-weapons-at-school cases) are real concerns, BUT counter-evidence is Hamel's explicit statement that 'plea agreements are generally NOT appropriate for domestic violence and DUI cases' (WAVY 9/9/2025) AND no documented Soros / Justice & Public Safety PAC / Real Justice PAC / Fair and Just Prosecution-letter signature — net null on q1 (mixed; not the Soros-prosecutor archetype the rubric singles out); (c) public_justice q2 — diversion-for-NONVIOLENT offenders (Drug/Recovery Court, Behavioral Health Court, veterans court; 'second largest Recovery Court in Virginia') does not directly contradict victim restitution / proportional punishment of evildoers (it addresses different offenders) → null. NO EVIDENCE on sanctity_of_life (was NOT a CA in 2020 so could not have signed the 2020 abortion-non-prosecution letter; no signature on 2022 Fair and Just Prosecution post-Dobbs joint statement; no individual statement located), biblical_marriage, family_child_sovereignty (juvenile-case prosecution record is mixed but doesn't map to school-choice/parental-notification/CRT cells), christian_liberty (Catholic parishioner is identity, no policy stance on q0/q2/q3/q4 located), economic_stewardship (statement that the CA's office is 'inadequately funded' is pro-spending posture but does not address deficit/balanced-budget rubric), election_integrity, public_justice q4 (no death-penalty / mandatory-minimums / asset-forfeiture statement located), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Chesapeake Sheriff's separately-elected ICE IGSA expansion is not Hamel's record and he did NOT publicly oppose it — silence is not a scoreable position here), self_defense.

3.note: Explicit Sept 17, 2025 statement framing violent crime in 'public health' terms: 'Breaking the cycle of violent crime requires treating the illness and not just the symptoms' — directly contradicts the rubric question of refusing to treat violent crime as a public-health excuse for diminished punishment.

profile.education: Booker T. Washington High School; B.S. Howard University (1982); M.D. Meharry Medical College (1989)
profile.background: Psychiatrist in Chesapeake since 1993 (Bon Secours Maryview / Chesapeake Regional Medical Center; professional name Patricia King-Jones MD); former Earth Science teacher; mother of three; lifelong Hampton Roads resident; founded a non-profit in 2020. Prior run: 2018 Chesapeake School Board (lost). Elected At-Large Nov 5, 2024 (3rd of 9, ~15.73%); sworn in Dec 30, 2024, took office Jan 1, 2025; term ends Dec 31, 2028. Regional service: Hampton Roads Planning District Commission (2025–). No public church/denominational affiliation located. Endorsed in 2024 by Chesapeake Democratic Committee, Sen. Louise Lucas (D), Del. Cliff Hayes (D), Chesapeake Education Association, Hampton Roads REALTORS, Phillip Jones (D mayoral candidate), and the Progressive Voters Guide of Virginia.

pat-king.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Chesapeake council member, sworn in 12/30/2024, term ends 12/31/2028. Party-in-practice openly Democratic by endorsement coalition (Chesapeake Democratic Committee, Sen. Louise Lucas D, Del. Cliff Hayes D, Phillip Jones for Mayor D, Chesapeake Education Association teachers' union, Hampton Roads REALTORS, Progressive Voters Guide of Virginia). VERY THIN ISSUE FOOTPRINT: 8 of 10 LOCAL rubric cells return NO direct citable statement; campaign website (patkingforchesapeake.com) has lapsed/expired and now redirects to unrelated domains, so direct campaign positions are not retrievable. TIMING: King took office Jan 1, 2025 — ALL named legacy Chesapeake votes (2017 sanctuary 8-1; Dec 10, 2019 unanimous 2A Constitutional City declaration; May 28, 2024 first-ever mayoral Pride proclamation; June 2024 6-3 ward-system referendum rejection; FY24 4-cent RE-tax cut; FY25 $1.6B budget; Nov 2024 curbside-recycling referendum) PREDATE her tenure and are not her record. Her advocacy to RESTORE a fee-bearing curbside-recycling program runs counter to opposing wasteful local spending but no FY26 rate-setting roll-call vote of hers has been located in available sources → economic_stewardship q2 left null pending a confirmed vote. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life (Progressive Voters Guide listing is general-progressive, not a single-issue abortion endorsement → null), biblical_marriage, family_child_sovereignty (Chesapeake Education Association endorsement is a teachers'-union labor endorsement, not a single-issue parental-rights/curriculum endorsement → null), christian_liberty (no invocation delivered, no church affiliation publicly stated), election_integrity, public_justice (no AFFIRMATIVE pro-LE statement located; her professional identity is psychiatry, which biases toward mental-health framing of public-safety questions), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration, self_defense. Confidence: evidence_local on the basis of party-in-practice + endorsement triangulation, but with HONEST nulls across the rubric. Flag: psychsearch.net reports a state-board reprimand on file for Dr. Patricia King, Chesapeake psychiatrist — context only, not scored.
